desc: Kandy is Sri Lanka's cultural highland city, known for temple heritage, lake views, and rail/road links into tea-country itineraries. It works well as a 2–4 day stop, with better reliability in drier months and higher rain risk through inter-monsoon periods.
hls: Temple city with strong cultural identity

todo.name: Temple of the Tooth at dusk
todo.desc: Visit Sri Dalada Maligawa for evening puja and lakeside walks afterward.

todo.name: Royal Botanic Gardens, Peradeniya
todo.desc: Spend a slow morning in shaded avenues, orchid houses, and giant bamboo groves.

todo.name: Kandy Lake + Bahirawakanda viewpoint
todo.desc: Pair a lake circuit with sunset city views from the hilltop Buddha statue.

todo.name: Train or road day trip to tea country
todo.desc: Use Kandy as a base for Nuwara Eliya, Hatton, or tea-factory stops.

todo.name: Traditional dance performance
todo.desc: Catch a Kandyan dance show in town before dinner.

todo.name: Market and street-food session
todo.desc: Browse Kandy market lanes for fruit, spices, and short-eat snacks.

wifi.note: Usually solid in town hotels/cafés, though heavy rain spells can reduce network stability.

prac.fltNote: Kandy has no commercial airport; most arrivals route via Colombo (CMB) then continue by road or rail. Return fare patterns to Sri Lanka are often ~£520–£790 off-peak, ~£790–£1,050 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,050+ during major holiday peaks.
prac.lang: Sinhala, Tamil, English widely used in tourism
prac.visa: Most visitors require an ETA/eVisa before arrival; confirm latest Sri Lanka entry requirements.
alerts: Road travel from Colombo can be lengthy in traffic; leave buffer for onward connections.
Temple entry times and dress expectations are strict — plan attire accordingly.
Rain-heavy months can affect visibility and day-trip reliability into surrounding highlands.

sweet: December to April is usually the most dependable window for temple visits and central-highland day trips.
